analysis.problem: Over 40% of people in Sub-Saharan Africa live in extreme poverty
analysis.root_cause: Lack of economic growth and unequal distribution of resources in Sub-Saharan Africa
leverage_points: International organizations and governments investing in economic development in Sub-Saharan Africa
Local governments implementing policies to reduce inequality and promote economic growth

proposed_actions.action: Invest in education and job training programs in Sub-Saharan Africa
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce poverty rate by 10% in the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Implement policies to promote fair trade and equal distribution of resources in Sub-Saharan Africa
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the GDP per capita by 20% in the next 10 years

evidence: More than 430 million people live in Sub-Saharan Africa, the poorest region in the world, where more than 40% of people lived in extreme poverty as of 2018
Poverty and inequality levels have worsened in Latin America and the Caribbean, and economic growth continues to fall short of its potential
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of planet, human, and cosmos, as poverty and inequality can have far-reaching consequences on the environment, human health, and the overall well-being of societies
analysis.summary: Over 40% of people in Sub-Saharan Africa live in extreme poverty, and lack of economic growth and unequal distribution of resources are the root causes of this problem. Investing in education and job training programs, and implementing policies to promote fair trade and equal distribution of resources can help reduce poverty and promote economic growth in the region.

raw_items.title: Sub-Saharan Africa - Wikipedia
raw_items.summary: Sub-Saharan Africa is a key player in the global minerals market, producing over 70% of the world's cobalt and hosting about 50% of its reserves in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). The region also harbors significant lithium deposits in Zimbabwe, DR Congo, and Mali.[100].
